steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Tom needs a mean score of at least 90 across four exams. The mean of four values equals their sum divided by 4.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The three known scores are 85, 78, and 98. Let $G$ be the fourth. The mean is $\frac{G + 85 + 78 + 98}{4}$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: "At least 90" means $\geq 90$, so the inequality is $\frac{G + 85 + 78 + 98}{4} \geq 90$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• A: $-(85 + 78 + 98) \le 4G$ — Negating the known scores is mathematically unrelated to computing a mean. Eliminated.
  • B: $G+85+78+98 \ge 360$ — Equivalent to the candidate after multiplying both sides by 4 (since $4 \times 90 = 360$), but this expression does not represent the mean directly as the question asks. Eliminated.
  • C: $\frac{(G + 85 + 78 + 98)}{4} \ge 90$ — Directly expresses "mean of all four exams is at least 90." That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
  • D: $\frac{(85 + 78 + 98)}{4} \ge 90 - 4G$ — Divides only three scores by 4 and moves $G$ incorrectly. Eliminated.
